submission.rationale: The 14:00 export-worker v2 deploy enabled long-running analytics transactions on db-primary that exhausted the shared connection pool (avg 44s export_worker txns, 100/100), which cascaded into the api-gateway 5xx/latency symptom, so RB-03 (cap/timeout long transactions and isolate the export-worker pool from the api pool) addresses the root cause — the TLS cert expiry was an unrelated internal-only co-incident.

The 14:00 export-worker v2 deploy enabled long-running analytics transactions on db-primary that exhausted the shared connection pool (avg 44s export_worker txns, 100/100), which cascaded into the api-gateway 5xx/latency symptom, so RB-03 (cap/timeout long transactions and isolate the export-worker pool from the api pool) addresses the root cause — the TLS cert expiry was an unrelated internal-only co-incident.

submission.reason: Cannot produce a correct, safe cutover plan because the migration spec/data dictionary state legacy.users.email is unique and legacy IDs map to new IDs via email with no collision handling, but the production sample contains duplicate emails for multiple legacy_ids: maria.vega@corp.com maps to 10442 and 88210 with different states, and d.okafor@corp.com maps to 20517 and 91003 with both active. submission.reason: The migration spec and data dictionary state legacy.users.email is unique/not null and that every legacy account maps exactly one-to-one to a new account by email with no collision handling, but the production sample contains duplicate emails mapped to multiple legacy_ids: maria.vega@corp.com appears for legacy_id 10442 and 88210 with different states, and d.okafor@corp.com appears for legacy_id 20517 and 91003 with both active. This makes the required legacy-ID-to-new-ID mapping by email ambiguous and unsafe, and the proposed post-migration unique constraint on new.accounts.email would collide.
submission.needed_from: Migration/data owner must define and approve duplicate-email resolution rules or correct/dedupe the production legacy.users data before cutover; database owner should confirm the final uniqueness constraint behavior after that resolution.
